name: Madison County Community Bank
description_short: Community bank with locations in Madison and Taylor County, Florida, offering personal checking, loans, and investment services with local ownership and decision-making.
description_long:
Madison County Community Bank was established in 1999 by local community members with a mission to serve the banking and financial needs of Madison County residents and businesses. In 2021, the bank expanded into Taylor County, opening a loan production office and subsequently launching Taylor County Community Bank as a division in January 2024. Both institutions operate as locally-owned banks with all board members, advisory board members, management, and staff residing and working in Madison, Taylor, and surrounding counties, ensuring that all banking decisions are made locally rather than at a distant corporate headquarters.

The bank offers a range of consumer and business banking products including personal checking accounts, personal loans, and investment and wealth management services. They maintain FDIC insurance backing all deposits with the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The institution provides modern banking conveniences alongside personal service, with mobile banking capabilities available through the MCCB mobile app for customers to manage accounts anytime and anywhere. Both locations feature lobby and drive-thru hours, with Taylor County Community Bank operating lobby hours 9 AM–4 PM Monday–Thursday and 9 AM–5 PM Friday, and drive-thru hours 8 AM–5 PM Monday–Friday.

What distinguishes Madison County Community Bank is its emphasis on local ownership and community connection. The bank explicitly markets itself as "Madison and Taylor's only locally owned bank" with a motto of "People you know… A Bank you can Trust." All major decisions are made locally by community members who live in the areas they serve, rather than by distant corporate entities. This localized approach contrasts with national and regional banking chains that dominate the market. The bank also demonstrates community commitment through educational resources on financial security, fraud prevention, and consumer protection.

The bank operates as a legitimate FDIC-insured financial institution with appropriate regulatory approvals (NMLS #403330) and appears to serve its stated community mission effectively. However, as a small regional bank with only two physical locations, it offers limited geographic accessibility compared to larger institutions. The website does not provide comprehensive rate information for loans or savings products, requiring prospective customers to contact the bank directly for specific terms. Customers seeking extensive branch networks or sophisticated digital-only banking experiences may find larger institutions more convenient. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Locally-owned bank with all decisions made by community members living in Madison and Taylor Counties
Established 26-year history (founded 1999) with recent expansion to second location in January 2024
Mobile banking app available for 24/7 account management
Offers personal loans, checking accounts, and investment/wealth management services
Convenient drive-thru hours (8 AM–5 PM Monday–Friday)
Provides free educational resources on fraud prevention, cybersecurity, and consumer protection
cons: Only two physical locations in rural Florida counties (Madison and Taylor), limiting accessibility
No rate, term, or product pricing information disclosed on website—requires direct contact
Limited information about loan products, APR ranges, or eligibility requirements
Small regional bank may lack sophisticated digital banking features of larger institutions
No mention of business banking products despite claims to serve local businesses
best_for: Residents of Madison and Taylor Counties, Florida seeking personalized local banking relationships
Small business owners in the region who prefer dealing with locally-based decision makers
Customers prioritizing community connection and personal service over national branch networks
Individuals who value knowing their bank's leadership and board members personally
diagnosis: Madison County Community Bank is best for residents and business owners in Madison and Taylor Counties, Florida who prioritize personal relationships, local decision-making, and community connection over the convenience of nationwide branch networks. The primary caveat is limited geographic accessibility (two locations only) and lack of transparent pricing information online, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for specific loan rates, terms, and product details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
